titleStmt.title: SII 36.261: original edition by P Shanmugam
No. 261. (A.R. No. 261 of 1910.) TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT. On the south wall of the central shrine in the Varāha Perumāḷ temple.

summary.p: Vallaṅkiḻāṉ Chāmichūṟṟi, a merchant of Mayilārppil, a taṉkūṟu in Puliyūrk-kōṭṭam, installed the image of goddess in the temple of Varāhadēva at Tiruviḍavandai, a dēvadāṉa in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu in Āmūrkkōṭṭam, and granted 15 kaḻañcus of gold for the supply of 90 nāḻis of ghee as interest to maintain one perpetual lamp. The sabha and the ūr assemblies of Tiruviḍavandai agreed to maintain the lamp. The village Mayilārppil could be identified with Mylapore now a part of the Chennai city.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī tirumakaḷ polap perunilac celviyun=taṉakke Urimai pūṇṭamai
manakkoḷa kān=taḷūrccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkai nāṭum kaṅka pāṭiyum nuḷampa pā
ṭiyun=taṭikai vaḻiyum kuṭamalai nāṭum kollamuṅ kaliṅkamum Eṇṭicai pukaḻtara Īḻa
maṇṭalamun=tiṇṭiṟal veṟṟi taṇṭaāṟk koṇṭa tanneḻil vaḷar Ūḻi Ellā
yāṇṭu toḻutakai vaḷarkkum yāṇṭe ceḻiyaraittumitta kovirāja kecarivarmarkku
yāṇṭu Eṭṭāvatu Āmūrk koṭṭattup paṭuvūr nāṭṭut tevataānan=tiruviṭavantai sapaiyo
mum Ūromuṅ kaiyyeḻuttu puliyūrk koṭṭattut tankūṟṟu mayilāṟppil Ūra
kattuc ceṭṭi viyāpāri vallaṅ kiḻān cāmicūṟṟi kaiyyāl yāṅkaḷ koṇṭu kaṭava po
n kācukallāl tuḷai niṟai poṉ patiṉaiṅ kaḻañcālum van=tapalicai Ēṟṟit to
ṇṇūṟṟu nāḻi Eṇṇaiyum Ivvūr Uṭaiya Āḻvār śrīvarākatevaṟkku Ivan tān
Eḻuntaruḷuvitta śrī baṣṭārakikku vaiytta tirunan=tāviḷakku Onṟiṉukku nicati mū Uḻakke
ṇṇaiyāka Āṇṭuvarai toṇṇūṟṟu nāḻi neyyeṇṇai cantrātittavaṟ
Aṭṭuvomānom Aṭṭomākil tanmāsanattil nicatam nāle kāṇan=taṇṭamiṭuva
tāka Iṭṭa taṇṭamiṟuttum Eṇṇai muṭṭāme Aṭṭuvommānom sabhaiyomum Ūro
mum Ivarkaḷ colla Eḻutinen Ivvūrp pirāhmaṇan pāmpūrt tāmotirak kiramavittanne
ṉ Itthanmattukku virotañ ceytār geṅkā kanniya tīrttattu naṭuvu ceytār ceyta pāvaṅ ko
ḷvār Itu śrīvaiṣṇavarakṣai
Digital edition of SII 36.261 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
